title: [lichen_l2.c:1141] rx_mutex trylock timeout recovery reinitializes potentially-held mutex
description:
Contract: If rx_mutex lock times out, assume RX thread was aborted holding it.
Violation: Lines 1141-1144 try to lock rx_mutex with 100ms timeout. If it fails, they reinitialize the mutex and lock forever. This is explicitly noted as 'defense-in-depth' but k_mutex_init() on a held mutex is undefined behavior.
Impact: The comment (lines 1135-1138) acknowledges this is for edge cases not caught by -ECANCELED check. In practice, if -ECANCELED was returned above, deinit() should have already reinitialized the mutex. This path catches 'kernel bugs or hardware faults'.
Mitigation: This is intentional UB-in-recovery-path tradeoff. Document that if this log message appears, system state is questionable.
close_reason: Duplicate of project-LICHEN-0li1.7 (same issue: rx_mutex reinit at lichen_l2.c:1141)
